title: Your Life in a Cartel at Every Rank (No Way Out)
hook: It's dawn, and you're cuffed at your own gate. The dogs went quiet an hour ago. Your phone stopped ringing at four. This is how it ends for everyone who reaches the top — you just never believed it would be you.
body: A cautionary POV climb through every rank of a cartel — you start as a thirteen-year-old lookout on a dust-town corner and rise to the man in the walled mountain compound, and learn the only two exits were always a cell or a grave. You live it in second person, present tense, level by level: EL HALCÓN, the lookout with a phone and a whistle and a plastic saint your mother ties on for luck → EL BURRERO, the mule riding the sierra with packages you're told never to open → EL SICARIO, the gunman they pay eighty-five dollars a body, the cheapest thing in the whole machine → JEFE DE PLAZA, the plaza boss who taxes an entire town under 'el piso' → EL LUGARTENIENTE, the lieutenant running the war until the dead stop being people → EL PATRÓN, the billion-dollar kingpin with a tunnel under the bedroom → LA RED, the network so big that taking out the kingpin doesn't move the price a single day → and the level above the throne: LA DEMANDA, the buyers up north who spend 150 billion dollars a year and never once learn your name. The twist the whole story is built on: you were never the kingpin. You were inventory — and inventory gets restocked. Dramatization for educational and entertainment purposes; the characters are fictional, the world is cautionary, and no criminal methods are shown. Watch the whole series — every life, every level.
pinned: You climb from a lookout worth $85 a job to a billion-dollar patrón — and learn you were inventory the whole time. At which level did the way out close?
poll: Where would YOU have gotten out — if there'd been a way? The corner / The first body / The plaza / There was never a way out
